docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Coral Island is a vibrant tropical farming simulation game developed by Stairway Games, released in October 2023 (version 1.0). Set on a gorgeous Indonesian-inspired archipelago, you inherit your grandmother's old farm and work alongside diverse villagers to restore the island's ecosystem and community.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer Stairway Games 📅 Release October 2023 (1.0) 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S

docs.title: 🌴 Farming & Ranching
docs.text: Coral Island's farming system is built around seasonal crops suited to a tropical climate. The soil starts depleted and must be cleared of trash and debris — reflecting the game's core theme of environmental restoration. You can raise animals including cows, chickens, goats, and sheep, each requiring outdoor pasture and daily care.

docs.title: Unique Mechanics
docs.text:
• Diving — Explore the ocean floor to clean trash, discover artifacts, and gather resources
  • Pollination — Bees and insect farms boost crop yields organically
  • Automation — Sprinklers and auto-feeders unlock as you progress through the town rank system

docs.title: 🤿 Diving & Ocean Restoration
docs.text: A standout feature of Coral Island is its underwater exploration. Using diving gear, you descend into the coral reefs surrounding the island to collect trash, salvage artifacts for the museum, and find rare gems. Cleaning the ocean raises your "Ocean Rank," which unlocks better diving equipment and reveals new areas.

docs.title: Ocean Rank Tiers
docs.text:
Rank Requirement Reward 🥉 Bronze C Clean 20 trash Basic fins upgrade 🥈 Silver B Clean 100 trash Oxygen tank upgrade 🥇 Gold A Donate 10 artifacts Underwater fast-travel

Tip: Prioritize upgrading your diving gear before your fishing rod. Diving provides significantly more early-game income and materials than fishing in Coral Island, and cleaning the ocean unlocks new areas packed with valuable forage.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early Spring: Plant Carrots and Radishes — fast turnaround for early cash flow.
  • Summer staple: Tomatoes are excellent early-regrowth crops for consistent income.
  • Fall jackpot: Pumpkins are the #1 profit crop in the game — plant as many as you can.
  • Winter farming: Crystal Cauliflower matches summer melons for profit/day.
  • Processing priority: Put Melon, Pumpkin, and Watermelon through Kegs for massive profit boosts.
  • Regrowth crops: Strawberry, Cranberry, and Frost Berry save replanting costs over long seasons.

Data source: Coral Island Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Arriving on the Island
docs.text: Your first day begins as you step off the boat into the charming town of Coral Island. You'll meet several villagers immediately — follow them to your grandparent's overgrown farm to begin. The tutorial walks you through clearing basic debris, meeting key NPCs, and planting your first seeds.

docs.title: Day 1 Priority List
docs.text:
1. Clear your farmhouse — Place your bed and storage chest inside
  2. Visit Sam's General Store — Buy the cheapest seeds available (parsnips or similar)
  3. Plant and water 6-8 crops — Don't overextend your stamina
  4. Talk to at least 5 villagers — Building relationships early pays off
  5. Check your mailbox — The tutorial quest rewards are inside

docs.title: 💰 Making Money Fast
docs.text:
In the first week, gold is tight. Your best money-makers are:

Method Estimated Daily Income Notes 🌾 Foraging 200-400g Wild fruits, flowers, and shells 🗑️ Trash cleanup 150-300g Sell scrap and recyclable materials 🎣 Fishing 300-600g Requires practice but reliable 🤿 Diving (from Day 3) 500-1000g Best income once unlocked

docs.title: 📅 First Week Schedule
docs.text:
Day Focus Day 1 Plant seeds, meet townsfolk, forage Day 2 Water crops, explore beach area, fish Day 3 Unlock diving — clean ocean trash for big money Day 4 Mine for ore to upgrade tools Day 5 Donate artifacts to museum, unlock new areas Day 6-7 Harvest first crops, reinvest in seeds

Tip: Do not sell raw ore in the first week — you need every scrap for tool upgrades. The pickaxe upgrade to copper should be your first priority, as it dramatically speeds up mining and debris clearing, saving you hours across the first season.

docs.title: Game Overview
docs.text:
• Developer: Giants Software
  • Platform: PC / PS5 / Xbox Series X|S
  • Genre: Farming Simulation
  • Features: Most realistic machinery simulation

docs.title: Day 1: Taking Over the Farm
docs.text:
1. Sell the starting tractor — too old, inefficient
  2. Rent a medium tractor (daily cost ≈ profit)
  3. Cultivate 3 small fields (about 5-10 acres)
  4. Plant soybeans (highest profit for beginners)

docs.title: Crop Profit Table
docs.text: Crop Growth Period Price/1000L Profit/Acre/Year Soybeans 4 months $3,800 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Corn 6 months $2,200 ⭐⭐⭐ Wheat 5 months $1,800 ⭐⭐ Canola 4 months $3,200 ⭐⭐⭐ Sunflowers 4 months $3,500 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Cotton 8 months $4,500 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.text: Order Investment Cost Benefit 1 Medium Tractor $150K Cultivation Efficiency +50% 2 Seeder + Harvester $200K Full Mechanization 3 Second Field $100K Double Output 4 Animal Barn $80K Steady Passive Income 5 Biogas Plant $500K Ultimate Passive Income

docs.text: Animal Purchase Cost Daily Profit Product Chickens $500 (10 pcs) $200 Eggs Pigs $5,000/head $300 Piglets Cows $3,000/head $500 Milk Sheep $2,000/head $250 Wool

docs.title: Money-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Rent, Don't Buy — Rent equipment instead of buying (save on repairs)
  2. Buy Land When Cheap — June has the lowest land prices
  3. Contract Work — Help NPCs farm to earn money
  4. Sell at Seasonal Peaks — Grain prices fluctuate heavily

docs.title: Fertilizer Effects
docs.text:
Fertilizations Yield Bonus Suggestion 0 times (no fertilizer) Base yield ❌ Wasting the field 1 time +15% ✅ At least 1 to start 2 times +30% ✅ Must-do at mid-game 3 times (perfect) +50% ⭐ Pursuing maximum yield

Core strategy: Save money with wheat as a beginner → pivot to sunflowers/canola mid-game → plant soybeans + grapes late-game. 2 fertilizations offer the best value; the marginal benefit of a 3rd is diminishing.

docs.title: Choosing a Map
docs.text:
Map Features Recommendation Riverbend Springs Beginner-friendly, orderly fields ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Hutan Pantai Southeast Asian style, small terraced fields ⭐⭐⭐ Zielonka European style, medium farms ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Recommended for beginners: Riverbend Springs — orderly fields, flat roads, and shops at a reasonable distance.

docs.title: Game Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Recommendation New Farmer Comes with basic equipment and fields, ready to go Best for beginners Farm Manager Plenty of funds, large fields, suitable for experienced players ❌ Don't pick as a beginner Start from Scratch Only empty land + small funds, buy everything yourself ❌ Try after some experience

docs.title: 1️⃣ Get Familiar with Controls
docs.text: Action Key (PC) Get in/out of vehicle F Start engine E Accelerate/Brake W/S Open menu Esc Map M Hire worker H Switch vehicle via Tab Tab

docs.title: 2️⃣ Check Your Assets
docs.text: Press M to open the map and check: - Where your fields are (green highlight) - Where your farm is (house icon) - Where the shop is (tool icon) - Where selling points are (different colored icons)

docs.title: 3️⃣ First Income
docs.text:
Fastest way to make money:

  1. Drive the tractor to your field
  2. Press V to switch tools → select plow or seeder
  3. Press H to hire a worker to help
  4. Drive another vehicle to haul goods for extra cash

docs.title: Crop Selection
docs.text:
Crop Growth Time Profit Good for Beginners 🌾 Wheat Fast Medium ✅ Best choice 🌽 Corn Medium Medium-High ✅ Steady 🌻 Sunflowers Fast Medium ✅ Plantable Barley Fast Low ❌ Low profit Canola Medium Medium ✅ Decent

Suggested first crop: Wheat — grows fast, steady profit, and can feed cows.

docs.title: Planting Process
docs.text:
Plow → Fertilize → Seed → Wait for Growth → Harvest → Sell
 |        |        |          |           |
Optional  Recommended  Required  Watch weather  Watch prices

docs.title: When to Upgrade Equipment?
docs.text: Signal Time to upgrade Always waiting for workers to finish ✅ Buy a second tractor Harvesting too slow ✅ Upgrade harvester No space to store grain ✅ Expand silo Earned $200,000+ ✅ Buy new land

docs.title: Recommended Purchase Order
docs.text:
1. Second tractor + trailer → haul goods for profit
2. Larger seeder → improve efficiency
3. Own harvester → no more renting
4. New land → expand scale
5. Animal pen → raise cows/sheep/chickens

docs.title: Raising Chickens Basics
docs.text: Chickens are the most cost-effective starting livestock: - ❌ Low cost (coop ~$5,000) - ✅ Collect eggs daily, guaranteed profit - ✅ Don't need much feed (free-range works) - ✅ Eggs auto-deliver to the coop, hassle-free

docs.title: ❓ Why did my worker stop moving?
docs.text: Possible issues: tree in the way, out of fuel, tool not lowered. Press H to re-hire.

docs.title: ❓ Not enough money?
docs.text:
• Take out a loan (Press P → Menu → Finances)
  • Do contract missions (blue icons on the map)
  • Start with cheap crops, don't buy the most expensive equipment right away

docs.title: ❓ How to sell grain?
docs.text:
1. Harvested grain is automatically stored in the silo
  2. Drive a trailer to the silo and press R to load
  3. Check which selling point has the best price on the map
  4. Drive there and press R to unload → get paid

docs.title: Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
Tip Description 🏃 Tab to Switch No need to get out, just Tab to switch to nearby vehicles 🗺️ Use Map to Check Prices Real-time prices at each selling point, sell to the highest 🤝 Do Contracts Earn money helping others, and practice with your equipment 💰 Price Fluctuations Wait for high prices to sell, don't rush to sell low 🚜 Rent vs Buy Buy frequently used equipment, rent occasional use

Core strategy: First plant wheat to save your seed money → buy a second vehicle for hauling → expand fields → raise chickens → buy a harvester → scale up planting. By your 3rd hour, you should have a steady cash flow.

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Complete Profit Guide
docs.text: Understanding crop profitability is the foundation of a successful farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide breaks down every major crop by revenue, cost, net profit per hectare, growing time, and seasonal considerations to help you maximize your bottom line.

docs.title: Crop Profitability Table
docs.text:
All figures are per hectare (ha) and assume average yield with standard fertilization (no precision farming bonuses). Prices fluctuate based on market demand — the values below represent typical mid-range prices.

When planning your crop rotation, profit per day (net profit ÷ growth time) gives the truest picture of efficiency.

docs.title: Seasonal Considerations
docs.text:
Farming Simulator 25 features a dynamic seasonal cycle that affects crop viability.

Season Best Crops to Plant Notes Spring (Mar–May) Wheat, Barley, Oats, Canola, Potatoes Prepare fields, fertilize before planting Summer (Jun–Aug) Corn, Soybeans, Sunflowers, Sorghum Fast growth, good weather windows Fall (Sep–Nov) Harvest most crops, plant winter wheat/barley Busiest season — plan equipment carefully Winter (Dec–Feb) Animal care, maintenance, logging Minimal field work; focus on animals and processing

docs.title: Rotation Strategy
docs.text:
A 3-field rotation maximizing profit:

  • Field 1: Potatoes (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Winter Wheat (Sep)
  • Field 2: Corn (Spring) → Harvest (Sep) → Fallow/Green Manure
  • Field 3: Soybeans (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Canola (Winter)

docs.title: Cost-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Fertilize efficiently — use precision farming to apply variable-rate fertilizer only where needed, saving 20–30% on input costs.
  2. Own your equipment — leasing is great for specialized harvesters, but buying tractors and basic tools pays off after 3 seasons.
  3. Sell at peak prices — crop prices fluctuate weekly. Store grain in silos and sell when prices are 15–20% above average.
  4. Use cover crops — planting grass or clover between cash crops reduces fertilizer needs by 50%.
  5. Lime management — test soil pH and apply lime only when required. Over-liming wastes money without additional yield benefit.
  6. Weed control — mechanical weeding (hoeing) costs less than herbicide and doesn't degrade soil quality.

docs.title: Animal Profitability
docs.text:
If you diversify into animal husbandry, here are the profit estimates per animal per year:

Animal Initial Cost Annual Profit Products Chicken $150 each $1,200/yr Eggs, Meat Sheep $500 each $2,500/yr Wool, Meat Cow (Dairy) $1,200 each $4,000/yr Milk, Meat Pig $400 each $3,000/yr Meat, Manure Horse $3,000 each $500/yr Breeding (harder to profit)

Best Animal Investment: Dairy cows offer the highest annual return, but require the most investment in barns and feeding equipment. Chickens are the easiest entry point with the lowest startup cost.

Data source: Farming Simulator Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Vehicle Comparison Guide
docs.text: Choosing the right tractor, harvester, or specialized vehicle is critical to running an efficient farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide compares key specifications including horsepower, top speed, price, and fuel capacity to help you make informed purchasing decisions.

docs.title: Purchasing Tips
docs.text:
1. Match tractor power to implement requirements — every tool has a minimum HP requirement. Undersized tractors work slowly; oversized tractors waste fuel.
  2. Consider fuel efficiency — Fendt tractors generally consume less fuel per HP/hour than competitors.
  3. Speed matters — a 50 km/h tractor covers fields faster than a 40 km/h model, saving real time on large maps.
  4. Don't overbuy — start with a medium tractor (140–150 HP) and a mid-range combine. Upgrade as your operation grows.
  5. Used vehicles — check the used vehicle dealer regularly for substantial discounts on older models.
  6. Lease before you buy — leasing a vehicle for a single contract is often cheaper than purchasing for infrequent use.

Data source: Farming Simulator Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Fields of Mistria — Complete Game Overview
About the Game
docs.text: Fields of Mistria is a farming simulation RPG developed by NPC Studio, released in August 2024 via Early Access on Steam. Set in a charming pastoral town recovering from a mysterious earthquake, players inherit a run-down farm and work to restore both the land and the nearby village.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / RPG / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer NPC Studio 📅 Release August 2024 (Early Access) 🖥️ Platform PC (Steam)

docs.title: 🪄 Magic & Skills
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming sims, Fields of Mistria introduces a magic system that evolves alongside your farming skills. Players learn spells tied to seasonal elements — water magic for irrigating crops, earth magic for tilling, and fire magic for smithing. The magic system ties directly into skill progression, with higher levels unlocking more powerful enchantments for tools and crops.

docs.title: Skill Categories
docs.text:
• 🌱 Farming — Plant, water, and harvest crops; magic-enhanced yields
  • ⛏️ Mining — Explore the mines beneath Mistria, collect ores and gems
  • 🎣 Fishing — Catch freshwater and ocean fish in various biomes
  • 🌿 Foraging — Gather wild ingredients and discover rare herbs
  • 🧙 Magic — Unlock and upgrade spells through practice and quests

docs.title: 🏘️ Town & NPCs
docs.text:
Mistria is home to a cast of fully voiced NPCs with deep relationship mechanics. Each villager has daily routines, seasonal events, and personal story arcs tied to the town's recovery. Building friendships unlocks new spells, recipes, and story progression.

Tip: Spend your first few days clearing only enough land to plant a small crop patch. Use the rest of your energy exploring town and talking to villagers — early relationships unlock important tool upgrades and magic abilities faster than rushing farm expansion.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early game: Plant Carrots (Spring) and Onions (Summer) — cheap and fast-growing for quick cash.
  • Mid game: Invest in Strawberries (Spring) and Cranberries (Fall) — regrowth crops give sustained income.
  • Late game: Watermelons (Summer) and Pumpkins (Fall) have the highest single-harvest profit.
  • Processing: Always run high-volume crops like Tomato, Corn, and Potato through the Preserves Jar for maximum value.
  • Seed Maker: Use on expensive regrowth crops (Strawberry, Cranberry) to save money on replanting.

docs.title: Fields of Mistria Fish Database — All Fish with Locations, Seasons, and Sell Prices
Fields of Mistria Fish Database
docs.text: Welcome to the complete fish database for Fields of Mistria. Whether you are a dedicated angler trying to complete the museum collection, a chef looking for the best ingredients, or a farmer seeking extra income, this guide covers every fish species currently available in the game. Use the tables below to find exactly where and when to catch each fish, along with their base sell prices.

docs.title: Understanding Fishing Mechanics
docs.text: Before diving into the species list, it helps to know the basics. Fishing in Fields of Mistria uses a timing-based minigame. Different fish appear in specific locations, seasons, times of day, and weather conditions. Your fishing level and the quality of your rod also affect what you can catch. Higher-quality fish (Silver, Gold, Iridium) sell for more and are worth more for cooking.

docs.title: Key Terms
docs.text:
• Location: The specific body of water or area where the fish is found.
  • Season: The in-game season(s) the fish is available.
  • Time: The in-game time window when the fish is active.
  • Weather: Some fish only appear in rain, sun, or storms.
  • Sell Price: The base price for a normal-quality fish. Prices increase with quality.

docs.title: Complete Fish List
docs.text: Below is the full database of fish organized by location. Each table includes all relevant catch conditions.

docs.title: Town River & Lake
docs.text:
The river running through the center of town and the small lake near the inn are the most accessible fishing spots for beginners.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Minnow Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 15g Carp Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 30g Perch Spring, Fall 6AM – 2PM Any 45g Sunfish Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 50g Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 80g Rainbow Trout Spring 6AM – 12PM Any 65g Bass Spring, Summer, Fall Any Any 55g Pike Fall 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 100g Eel Spring, Fall 6PM – 2AM Rain 70g Goldfish Spring, Summer 10AM – 4PM Sunny 120g

docs.title: Mistria Beach & Ocean
docs.text:
The ocean to the south of town offers saltwater species. You will need the Fishing Rod Upgrade from the blacksmith to cast far enough to reach deep water fish.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Sardine Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 6PM Any 40g Herring Spring, Winter 6AM – 2PM Any 50g Flounder Spring, Summer 6AM – 7PM Any 75g Tuna Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 110g Red Snapper Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Rain 130g Halibut Spring, Fall 6AM – 11AM, 4PM – 7PM Any 150g Lobster Summer, Fall Any Any 200g Crab Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 180g Octopus Summer 6PM – 2AM Any 250g Shark Summer 6AM – 9AM, 4PM – 7PM Sunny 350g Sea Turtle Summer, Fall 10AM – 4PM Sunny 400g

docs.title: Mountain Lake
docs.text:
The lake in the northern mountain area is home to cold-water and rare species. Access requires clearing the path north of the farm.

Fish Name Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Largemouth Bass Spring, Summer, Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 60g Walleye Fall, Winter 12PM – 2AM Rain 105g Sturgeon Summer, Winter 6AM – 7PM Any 180g Salmon Fall 6AM – 7PM Any 120g Trout Spring, Summer 6AM – 12PM Any 70g Bluegill Spring, Summer 9AM – 4PM Sunny 45g Lake Catfish Summer, Fall 6PM – 2AM Any 90g Ghost Fish Winter 6PM – 2AM Any 220g Ice Carp Winter 6AM – 7PM Snow 160g Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) Spring 6AM – 9AM Rain 1,000g

docs.title: Secret Fishing Spots
docs.text:
There are a few hidden fishing locations that require specific quests or tools to access. These spots contain unique fish.

Fish Name Location Season Time Weather Base Sell Price Lava Eel Volcanic Cave (floor 10+) Any Any Any 450g Crystal Carp Crystal Caverns Any Any Any 300g Moon Jellyfish Mistria Lake (during Festival of Stars) Fall 28 8PM – 12AM Clear 500g Void Fish Sewers (unlocked after museum donation milestone) Any Any Any 600g

docs.title: Fish Uses
docs.text:
Fish are not just for selling. They have several important uses in Fields of Mistria:

  • Cooking: Many recipes require specific fish. Cooked meals provide energy, health, and temporary buffs.
  • Museum Donations: The museum curator accepts one of each species. Donating all fish unlocks special rewards.
  • Gifting: Some villagers love certain fish. For example, the fisherman NPC loves Catfish and Pike.
  • Fish Ponds: You can raise fish in ponds on your farm. They produce roe, which can be aged and sold for profit.
  • Quests: The town bulletin board occasionally requests specific fish for rewards.

docs.title: Best Fish for Profit
docs.text:
If your goal is to make gold, focus on these high-value fish:

  1. Legendary Fish (The Ancient One) – 1,000g (Spring, Rain, Mountain Lake)
  2. Moon Jellyfish – 500g (Fall 28 only)
  3. Lava Eel – 450g (Volcanic Cave)
  4. Sea Turtle – 400g (Summer/Fall, Beach)
  5. Shark – 350g (Summer, Beach)

For consistent daily income, Catfish (80g) and Red Snapper (130g) are reliable options that are easier to catch.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Upgrade your rod early: Better rods let you cast farther and catch rarer fish.
  • Use bait: Craft bait from bug meat to reduce bite time and increase catch rate.
  • Check the weather: Rain and snow unlock exclusive species. Always fish during bad weather.
  • Fish at different times: Many fish are only active at dawn, dusk, or night. Set reminders to check each time window.
  • Complete the museum: The rewards for donating all fish include a permanent fishing buff and a unique decoration.
  • Cook before selling: Some fish sell for more when cooked into dishes. Check the recipe book before selling raw fish.
  • Build a fish pond: Once you have a surplus of high-value fish like Sturgeon or Lava Eel, place them in a pond for passive roe income.

docs.title: Your First Day
docs.text: When you arrive at your overgrown farm in Mistria, the first priority is survival. You'll start with basic tools — a watering can, hoe, axe, and pickaxe — and a small patch of arable land.

docs.title: Day 1 Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Clear weeds and small debris from 6-8 farm tiles
  • [x] Visit the general store in town for starter seeds
  • [x] Plant your first crops and water them
  • [x] Meet at least 3 villagers to start building relationships
  • [x] Check the town notice board for available quests

docs.title: 🌾 First Season Strategy
docs.text: Spring (Season 1) is about building a foundation. Focus on crops with short grow times — turnips and spring greens mature in 4-5 days and provide quick gold to reinvest. Avoid large fields until you have the stamina to water them daily.

docs.title: Recommended Early Crops
docs.text: Crop Grow Time Sell Price Notes Turnip 4 days 60g Fastest turnaround Spring Green 5 days 90g Good early profit Tomato 7 days 120g Regrows after harvest

docs.title: 🧙 Unlocking Magic Early
docs.text:
Magic unlocks around day 5 after you complete your first town quest. To speed this up:

  1. Complete the "Clearing the Path" quest by removing large debris
  2. Talk to Valen, the town elder, after clearing 10 debris tiles
  3. Accept the Spell Font quest to learn your first water spell
  4. Use magic to water crops — it covers a 3×3 area and saves stamina

Tip: Always keep 200-300g in reserve for seed purchases. A common beginner mistake is spending all gold on tool upgrades before securing a steady crop income. Prioritize reinvesting in seeds until you have at least 10 planted tiles producing consistently.

docs.title: Palia — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text:
Optimize your garden layout and crop selection in Palia with this complete profit guide. Unlike most farming games, Palia uses a garden-plot grid system where crops can be planted individually or in rows, and quality (star value) affects sell prices.

Note: All prices shown are base (non-star) quality. Star-quality crops sell for roughly 1.5× base price. Gardening skill level and fertilizer can also boost yield and quality.

docs.title: Crop Profit Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Price (Gold) Growth (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price Profit/Plot Carrot 10 24h 1 18 8 Potato 15 48h 1 33 18 Tomato 20 48h 2 16 each 12 Onion 12 36h 1 25 13 Corn 25 60h 2 20 each 15 Rice 15 36h 1 28 13 Wheat 10 24h 1 20 10 Blueberry 30 72h 3 15 each 15 Cotton 20 48h 2 18 each 16 Pepper 18 48h 2 15 each 12 Lettuce 8 18h 1 14 6 Cabbage 22 48h 1 45 23 Bok Choy 14 30h 1 28 14 Spinach 12 24h 1 24 12 Ginger 35 72h 1 65 30 Mushroom 30 72h 2 28 each 26

docs.title: Fast Crops (24 hours or less)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Lettuce 18h 6 0.33 Carrot 24h 8 0.33 Wheat 24h 10 0.42 Spinach 24h 12 0.50

docs.title: Medium Crops (36–48 hours)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Onion 36h 13 0.36 Bok Choy 30h 14 0.47 Rice 36h 13 0.36 Potato 48h 18 0.38 Tomato 48h 12 0.25 Cabbage 48h 23 0.48 Cotton 48h 16 0.33 Pepper 48h 12 0.25 Corn 60h 15 0.25

docs.title: Slow Crops (72+ hours)
docs.text: Crop Growth Profit/Plot Profit/Hour Blueberry 72h 15 0.21 Ginger 72h 30 0.42 Mushroom 72h 26 0.36

docs.title: Star Quality Value Multiplier
docs.text:
Quality Price Multiplier Example: Carrot ⭐ Base (No Star) 1.0× 18 Gold ⭐⭐ Star Quality ~1.5× 27 Gold ⭐⭐⭐ Rare Star ~2.0× 36 Gold

Star quality is random but can be increased with: - Quality Up Fertilizer — Boosts star quality chance by 25% - Gardening Skill Level — Each level slightly increases base quality chance - Special Soil Plots — Some home plot layouts have natural quality bonuses

docs.title: Gardening Level Rewards
docs.text: Level Unlock 1 Basic Soil Plots (3×3), Carrot Seeds 2 Potato Seeds, Watering Can Upgrade 3 Onion Seeds, Cotton Seeds 4 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 4th row) 5 Tomato Seeds, Quality Up Fertilizer 6 Rice Seeds, Bok Choy Seeds 7 Extra Soil Plot (unlock 5th row) 8 Corn Seeds, Pepper Seeds 9 Blueberry Seeds, Mushroom Seeds 10 Ginger Seeds, Deluxe Fertilizer

docs.title: Best Crops for Each Play Style
Early Game (Levels 1–4)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Carrot Fastest turnaround, cheap seeds 2nd Wheat Quick cash + cooking ingredient 3rd Onion Decent profit, 36h growth 4th Potato Best early profit-per-plot

docs.title: Mid Game (Levels 5–8)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Cabbage Best profit-per-plot at 48h 2nd Bok Choy Fast medium profit 3rd Cotton High yield (2 per harvest) 4th Corn Good multi-harvest crop

docs.title: Late Game (Levels 9–10)
docs.text: Priority Crop Why 1st Ginger Highest profit-per-plot (30 Gold) 2nd Mushroom Second best at 26 Gold/plot 3rd Blueberry Multi-harvest, good for gifts 4th Cabbage Still top-tier for 48h cycle

docs.title: Gardening Tips
docs.text:
• Watering: Crops only grow when watered. Unwatered crops pause growth but don't die.
  • Weeds: Weeds spawn randomly on unplanted soil plots. Remove them before they spread.
  • Fertilizer: Quality Up Fertilizer is best for high-base-price crops (Ginger, Cabbage, Mushroom).
  • Multi-harvest: Tomato, Corn, Cotton, Pepper, and Blueberry produce multiple items per harvest — great for cooking ingredients.
  • Gifting: Star-quality Blueberries are a liked gift for many NPCs; Star-quality Sweet Leaf is a weekly want for some.
  • Plot expansion: Invest in extra soil plots as soon as you level up — more plots = more gold.

docs.title: Weekly Profit Estimate (20 Plots)
docs.text:
Strategy Crops Est. Gold/Week Fast Cycle Lettuce, Carrot, Spinach ~900 Balanced Cabbage, Potato, Onion, Bok Choy ~1,600 High Value Ginger, Mushroom, Blueberry ~2,200 Star Focus Ginger (Quality Fertilizer) ~3,000+

Data source: Palia Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Palia Crops Database — All Crop Types with Grow Times, Seed Costs, and Profit per Harvest
Palia Crops Database
docs.text: Gardening is one of the most rewarding and profitable skills in Palia. Whether you are a new player looking to make your first gold or a seasoned farmer optimizing your plot for maximum efficiency, understanding each crop’s grow time, seed cost, and profit per harvest is essential. This database covers every crop currently available in Palia, organized by category, with detailed tables and practical tips to help you plan your farm.

docs.title: Crop Categories Overview
docs.text: Palia crops are divided into three main categories: Vegetables, Fruits, and Special Crops. Each category has unique growth mechanics, seed costs, and harvest yields. Vegetables are the most common and form the backbone of any farm. Fruits require more time but often yield higher profits per harvest. Special crops include rare or event-specific plants that can be used for cooking, gifting, or selling.

docs.title: Vegetable Crops
docs.text:
Vegetables are the foundation of Palia gardening. They grow quickly, have lower seed costs, and are excellent for generating consistent income. Below is the complete list of vegetable crops with their key stats.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Carrot 10 6 2 15 20 Potato 15 8 2 25 35 Onion 20 10 2 35 50 Tomato 25 12 3 30 65 Corn 30 14 2 50 70 Pepper 35 16 2 60 85 Rice 40 18 2 75 110 Wheat 20 8 3 20 40

Notes on Vegetables: - Carrots and Potatoes are ideal for early-game gold due to their short grow times. - Tomatoes and Corn offer the best profit per hour when planted in bulk. - Wheat is unique because it yields 3 units per harvest, making it excellent for cooking recipes.

docs.title: Fruit Crops
docs.text:
Fruits take longer to grow but produce higher-value items. Many fruits are also used in advanced cooking recipes or as gifts for villagers. Fruit crops often require trellises or specific garden layouts.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Blueberry 50 24 2 100 150 Apple 60 30 2 120 180 Peach 70 36 2 150 230 Cherry 80 40 2 180 280 Grape 90 48 3 130 300

Notes on Fruits: - Grapes are the most profitable fruit per harvest, but they require the longest grow time. - Blueberries are a great mid-game crop with a reasonable 24-hour grow time. - Fruits are often used in juice-making or jam recipes, which can double their value.

docs.title: Special Crops
docs.text:
Special crops include rare seeds obtained from events, quests, or high-level gardening. These crops often have unique properties, such as being used for special recipes or having a chance to produce multiple harvests.

Crop Name Seed Cost (Gold) Grow Time (Hours) Harvest Yield Sell Price per Unit Profit per Harvest Starfruit 150 72 1 500 350 Mushroom 100 48 2 200 300 Spice Sprout 80 36 2 160 240 Cotton 40 20 3 50 110

Notes on Special Crops: - Starfruit is the highest-value single crop but has a long grow time and high seed cost. It is best used for gifting or cooking. - Mushrooms are a reliable source of income for mid-to-late game players. - Cotton is essential for crafting fabric and furniture, making it valuable beyond just selling.

docs.title: Profit Analysis and Best Crops by Strategy
Best Crops for Early Game (First 20 Hours)
docs.text:
If you are just starting out, focus on crops with low seed costs and fast grow times. Carrots and Potatoes are your best friends. Plant as many as your energy allows, and reinvest your profits into more seeds.

  • Carrot: 6-hour grow time, 20 gold profit per harvest. You can plant and harvest 4 times in a single day.
  • Potato: 8-hour grow time, 35 gold profit per harvest. Slightly better profit per hour than carrots.

docs.title: Best Crops for Mid Game (20-50 Hours)
docs.text:
Once you have a steady gold flow, switch to higher-profit crops like Tomatoes, Corn, and Blueberries. These crops offer better returns per garden plot.

  • Tomato: 12-hour grow time, 65 gold profit per harvest. Great for cooking and selling.
  • Blueberry: 24-hour grow time, 150 gold profit per harvest. Perfect for daily harvest cycles.

docs.title: Best Crops for Late Game (50+ Hours)
docs.text:
Late-game players should focus on Grapes, Starfruit, and Mushrooms. These crops require patience but yield the highest profits and are essential for end-game recipes.

  • Grape: 48-hour grow time, 300 gold profit per harvest. Best profit per plot.
  • Starfruit: 72-hour grow time, 350 gold profit per harvest. Ideal for special dishes.

docs.title: Watering and Fertilizer
docs.text: All crops require regular watering. You can use a watering can or build sprinklers to automate the process. Fertilizer can be applied to reduce grow time or increase yield. Quality fertilizer reduces grow time by 20%, while Rich Fertilizer increases harvest yield by 1 unit.

docs.title: Crop Rotation and Soil Quality
docs.text: Soil quality degrades over time. Planting the same crop repeatedly reduces yield. Rotate your crops to maintain soil health. Using fertilizer also helps replenish soil nutrients.

docs.title: Multi-Harvest Crops
docs.text: Some crops, like Tomatoes and Grapes, can be harvested multiple times. After the initial harvest, the plant will regrow in half the original grow time. This makes them extremely efficient for long-term farming.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Start small: Focus on Carrots and Potatoes until you have at least 500 gold.
  • Automate early: Build sprinklers as soon as possible to save time and energy.
  • Use fertilizer: Quality Fertilizer on high-value crops like Grapes can significantly boost your daily profit.
  • Sell raw or cook: Cooking ingredients into meals often doubles or triples their value. Check recipes before selling.
  • Gift wisely: Some villagers love specific crops. Gifting a Starfruit to a villager can increase friendship faster than any other gift.
  • Plan your layout: Leave space for trellises if you plan to grow fruits like Grapes. A well-organized garden saves time and maximizes space.
  • Check the market: Crop prices fluctuate daily. Sell when prices are high for maximum profit.

docs.title: Palia Fish Database — Complete Fish Catalog with Locations, Bait Requirements, and Bundle Info
Palia Fish Database — Complete Fish Catalog
docs.text: Welcome to the most comprehensive Palia fish database. Whether you’re a seasoned angler looking to complete your collection or a new player trying to finish a bundle, this guide covers every fish in the game. We’ve organized everything by location, bait type, and bundle relevance so you can spend less time guessing and more time reeling in the big ones.

docs.title: How Fishing Works in Palia
docs.text:
Fishing in Palia is a core skill that rewards you with food ingredients, quest items, and valuable resources. Each body of water has its own unique fish pool. Your success depends on three main factors:

  • Location: Rivers, lakes, ponds, and coastal waters each host different species.
  • Bait: Standard worms work for most fish, but glow worms and special bait are required for rare and epic catches.
  • Time of Day: Some fish only appear during the day (6:00–18:00), others at night (18:00–6:00), and a few are active 24/7.

You can also use fishing hooks and buffs from food to increase your catch rate, but the tables below assume no modifiers.

docs.title: Complete Fish Table by Location
docs.text: Below is the master table of every fish in Palia, sorted by the region where you can catch them. Rarity is indicated as Common (C), Uncommon (U), Rare (R), or Epic (E).

docs.text: Fish Name Rarity Bait Time Bundle? Silver Carp C Worm Any No Bluegill C Worm Day No Catfish U Worm Night Yes (Fisherman’s) Painted Perch U Worm Day No Kilima Sturgeon R Glow Worm Any Yes (Master Angler) Void Ray E Glow Worm Night Yes (Night Sky)

docs.title: Bundle Information
docs.text: Bundles are a major endgame goal in Palia. Completing them rewards you with unique furniture, recipes, and progression items. Here’s exactly which fish you need for each bundle.

docs.title: Fisherman’s Bundle
docs.text:
• Catfish (Kilima River, Night, Worm)
  • Longnose Gar (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional uncommon fish of your choice

Reward: Fishing Rod Stand (decorative item)

docs.title: Oceanic Bundle
docs.text:
• Giant Stingray (Kilima Coast, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Coral Trout (Bahari Coast, Day, Glow Worm)
  • 1 additional ocean fish of uncommon or higher rarity

Reward: Seashell Chime (wall decoration)

docs.title: Night Sky Bundle
docs.text:
• Void Ray (Kilima River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Midnight Pike (Bahari River, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Glowing Koi (Underground Lake, Night, Glow Worm)

Reward: Starry Night Lantern (light source)

docs.title: Master Angler Bundle
docs.text:
• Kilima Sturgeon (Kilima River, Any, Glow Worm)
  • Ancient Fish (Kilima Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Mythic Fish (Bahari Coast, Night, Glow Worm)
  • Crystal Carp (Underground Lake, Any, Glow Worm)

Reward: Master Angler Trophy (display item) + 5,000 Gold

docs.title: Bait Guide
docs.text:
Knowing which bait to use saves you time and resources.

  • Worm Bait: Standard bait. Catches all common and uncommon fish. Can be dug from the ground or purchased from the General Store for 10 Gold each.
  • Glow Worm Bait: Required for all rare and epic fish. Crafted using 1 Worm Bait + 1 Brightshroom. Brightshrooms are found in caves and near water at night.
  • Special Bait: Some limited-time events introduce unique bait, but glow worms are your primary tool for high-tier fishing.

Pro Tip: Always carry at least 50 glow worms when heading to Bahari Bay or the Underground. Rare fish appear more frequently in these zones, and you don’t want to waste a trip.

docs.title: Fishing Skill Progression
docs.text:
Your fishing level increases as you catch fish. Higher levels unlock new recipes, better rods, and the ability to catch rarer fish without as much struggle.

Level Unlock 1 Basic Rod, Worm Bait 3 Standard Rod (longer cast) 5 Glow Worm Bait recipe 7 Fine Rod (better reel speed) 10 Exquisite Rod (best stats) 15 Fishing Hook upgrades available

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Always fish at night for epics. Every epic fish in Palia (except Crystal Carp) is only available between 18:00 and 6:00. Plan your fishing trips accordingly.
  2. Use glow worms for bundles. If you’re working on bundles, skip worm bait entirely. Glow worms are the only way to catch the rare and epic fish required.
  3. Fish in the Underground for variety. The Underground Lake is the only place to catch Glowing Koi and Crystal Carp, both needed for bundles. It’s also a great spot for leveling up.
  4. Don’t sell rare fish. Before selling any fish, check if it’s needed for a bundle. You’ll save yourself hours of frustration later.
  5. Eat fishing buff food. Dishes like Fisherman’s Brew (+2 Fishing) and Spicy Fish Stew (+3 Fishing) increase your catch rate and reduce the time fish take to bite. Always cook these before a serious fishing session.
  6. Upgrade your rod first. Spend your early gold on the Standard Rod, then the Fine Rod. The longer cast and faster reel speed make a huge difference when chasing rare fish.

Happy fishing, and may your line always be tight! For more Palia guides, check out our crop database and crafting recipes.

docs.title: Complete Friendship & Romance Guide for Palia — All NPC preferences, weekly wants, romance levels, and fastest path to max relationships
Complete Friendship & Romance Guide for Palia
docs.text: Building relationships with the villagers of Palia is a core part of the game. Not only does it unlock heartwarming cutscenes and lore, but it also grants you essential recipes, furniture, and even the ability to romance multiple characters. This guide covers everything you need to know: from NPC gift preferences and weekly wants to romance levels and the fastest path to maxing out every relationship. For other Palia resources, check our Crops Guide, Fish Database, or Crop Profit Calculator.

docs.title: Beginner Tips for Friendship & Romance
docs.text:
Before diving into the specifics, here are the golden rules for efficient relationship building in Palia:

  1. Talk to Everyone Daily: Speaking to an NPC once per day gives a small friendship boost. It’s free and adds up over time.
  2. Gift Weekly Wants First: Every NPC has a "Weekly Want" that changes every Monday. Gifting this item gives a massive friendship bonus (usually 3x a normal liked gift). Prioritize these.
  3. Know Their Loves vs. Likes: "Love" gifts give the most points, "Like" gifts give moderate points, and "Dislike" gifts give nothing or negative points. Never give a disliked item.
  4. Use Cake Parties for High-Value Gifts: The best universal "Love" gifts are often high-tier cooked meals. Join a "Cake Party" (a player-organized event on the Palia Discord or in-game chat) to mass-produce cakes like Celebration Cake or Bundled Blueberry Pie with other players sharing ingredients and labor.
  5. Romance is Separate from Friendship: You can max friendship (Level 4) with everyone. Romance (Level 1-4) is an additional track that requires you to first reach friendship level 3 and then give a "Heartdrop Lily" to initiate.
  6. You Can Romance Everyone: Palia allows polyamory. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously without jealousy penalties.
  7. Focus on One NPC at a Time: For fastest results, focus your high-value gifts on one NPC per day until they are maxed, then move to the next.

docs.title: Friendship Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock basic dialogue and the ability to give gifts.
  • Level 2: Unlock the NPC's first friendship quest and a small reward (e.g., recipe, furniture).
  • Level 3: Unlock the ability to initiate romance (requires a Heartdrop Lily). Also unlocks a second friendship quest.
  • Level 4: Max friendship. Unlocks the final friendship quest and a significant reward (e.g., a rare recipe, a plushie, or a writ).

docs.title: Romance Levels (1-4)
docs.text:
• Level 1: Unlock the "Flirt" dialogue option and a pin.
  • Level 2: Unlock a romance-specific quest and a unique reward (e.g., a wall decoration).
  • Level 3: Unlock a second romance quest and a more personal reward (e.g., a portrait).
  • Level 4: Max romance. Unlocks the final romance quest and the ultimate reward (e.g., a unique outfit, a special emote, or a furniture set).

docs.title: All NPC Gift Preferences & Weekly Wants
docs.text:
Below is the master table of every NPC, their universally loved gifts, and their typical weekly want categories. Note: Weekly wants are randomized each week, but they always fall into one of the categories listed below.

NPC Universal Love (Best Gift) Universal Like (Good Gift) Weekly Want Category Auni Glow Worm, Any Fish Bugs, Foraged Items Bugs, Fish Ashura Grilled Meat, Any Sushi Meat Dishes, Mushrooms Cooked Meat Dishes Badruu Pickled Potatoes, Any Cake Vegetables, Seeds Cooked Vegetable Dishes Caleri Any Book (e.g., The Lost Chapter), Starstone Gatherables, Gems Gatherables, Books Chayne Heartdrop Lily, Any Tea Foraged Herbs, Mushrooms Foraged Items, Tea Delaila Celebration Cake, Any Pie Eggs, Milk, Butter Cooked Desserts Einar Any Fish (especially rare ones), Glow Worm Fishing Lures, Shells Fish, Fishing Supplies Elouisa Any Mushroom, Any Rare Bug Bugs, Gatherables Bugs, Mushrooms Eshe Any Gem (e.g., Emerald, Garnet), Starstone High-value Gatherables Gems, Starstones Hassian Any Meat Dish (e.g., Spicy Rice), Any Sushi Meat, Hunting Lures Cooked Meat Dishes Hekla Any Fish Dish (e.g., Fish Stew), Any Gem Fish, Gems Cooked Fish Dishes Hodari Any Sushi, Any Fish Dish Fish, Mining Resources Cooked Fish Dishes Jel Any Fabric (e.g., Silk, Cotton), Any Rare Gem Gatherables, Thread Fabric, Gems Jina Any Book, Any Gem Gatherables, Fossils Books, Gems Kenyatta Any Cake, Any Sweet (e.g., Candy) Cooked Desserts, Flowers Cooked Desserts Nai'o Any Meat Dish, Any Sushi Meat, Vegetables Cooked Meat Dishes Najuma Any Gem, Any Rare Ore (e.g., Palium) Mining Resources, Gems Gems, Ores Reth Any Cooked Meal (especially high-tier), Any Cake Ingredients, Vegetables Cooked Meals Sifuu Any Sushi, Any Meat Dish Meat, Foraged Items Cooked Meat Dishes Tamala Any Rare Flower, Any Tea Foraged Herbs, Gatherables Flowers, Tea Tau Any Meat Dish, Any Sushi Meat, Hunting Lures Cooked Meat Dishes Tish Any Furniture (especially crafted), Any Flower Gatherables, Decor Furniture, Flowers Zeki Any Gem, Any Rare Item (e.g., Plushie) Gatherables, Coins Gems, Rare Items

docs.title: Step 1: Stockpile High-Value Gifts
docs.text:
The single fastest way to gain friendship is to always give a "Love" gift. The best universal loves are:

  • Celebration Cake: Loved by almost everyone (Delaila, Kenyatta, Reth, Badruu, etc.).
  • Any Sushi: Loved by Ashura, Hassian, Hodari, Nai'o, Sifuu, Tau.
  • Any Gem (e.g., Emerald, Garnet): Loved by Eshe, Hekla, Jel, Jina, Najuma, Zeki.
  • Heartdrop Lily: Loved by Chayne and required to initiate romance.

Strategy: Focus on cooking high-tier meals. Join a Cake Party to get 50+ Celebration Cakes in one session. Alternatively, check the Palia Fish Database for rare fish locations to make sushi.

docs.title: Step 2: Prioritize Weekly Wants
docs.text:
Every Monday, check each NPC's weekly want. Gifting this item gives 3x the friendship points of a normal liked gift. If the weekly want is also a "Love" item, you get an even bigger boost.

Example: If Delaila's weekly want is "Any Pie" and you give her a Celebration Cake (which is a pie), you get a massive bonus.

docs.title: Step 3: Daily Routine
docs.text:
1. Talk to everyone (takes 5 minutes).
  2. Gift the weekly want to 2-3 NPCs per day (use your best items).
  3. Gift a "Love" item to the remaining NPCs.
  4. Complete quests as they appear. Quests give huge friendship boosts.

docs.title: Step 4: Romance Initiation
docs.text:
Once you reach friendship level 3 with an eligible NPC, you need a Heartdrop Lily to start the romance track. Heartdrop Lilies are rare flowers found in Bahari Bay (especially near the flooded steps) or purchased from the Underground Market (Zeki's secret shop) for 500 Gold each.

Tip: Farm Heartdrop Lilies by planting them in your garden. They take 5 days to grow but can be harvested repeatedly.

docs.text:
Here is every reward you get for reaching each romance level with each NPC.

docs.title: Advanced Strategy: The "Cake Party" Method
docs.text:
This is the most efficient way to max all relationships.

  1. Gather Ingredients: You need Sweet Leaf, Blueberry, Wheat, Egg, Milk, and Butter.
  2. Join a Cake Party: Use the Palia Discord or in-game chat to find a group. Usually, one person provides the ingredients, others provide the fuel (coal) and cooking skills.
  3. Mass Produce: You can make 50-100 Celebration Cakes in one session.
  4. Distribute: Give one Celebration Cake to every NPC who loves it (most of them). This single gift will often jump them from Level 1 to Level 2 instantly.

Pro Tip: Save your Celebration Cakes for NPCs whose weekly want is a "Cooked Dessert" for an even bigger boost.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text:
Q: Can I lose friendship or romance progress? A: No. Once gained, friendship and romance points never decay. You cannot lose levels.

Q: How many gifts can I give per day? A: You can give one gift per NPC per day (resets at 6:00 AM Palia time).

Q: Do I need to complete quests to progress romance? A: Yes. Each romance level requires completing a specific quest given by the NPC. These quests often involve gathering rare items or cooking specific meals.

Q: Can I date multiple people at once? A: Yes! Palia has no jealousy system. You can romance every eligible NPC simultaneously.

Q: What is the fastest way to get Heartdrop Lilies? A: Plant them in your garden. They are a perennial crop, meaning they regrow after harvest. Also check Zeki's Underground Market daily.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text:
Maxing out friendships and romances in Palia is a marathon, not a sprint, but with the right strategy, you can significantly speed up the process. Focus on weekly wants, stockpile high-value cooked meals, and talk to everyone daily. The rewards—from unique outfits to heartwarming cutscenes—are well worth the effort.

Now go out there, make some friends (and lovers), and enjoy all that Palia has to offer!

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock — Complete Game Overview
🏜️ My Time at Sandrock — Game Overview
About the Game
docs.text: My Time at Sandrock is an open-world crafting RPG developed by Pathea Games, set in the same universe as My Time at Portia. Released in November 2023, this sequel moves from Portia's lush valley to a harsh post-apocalyptic desert town. As a new Builder, you must revitalize Sandrock by crafting machinery, building relationships, and fending off environmental threats.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Crafting RPG / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player + Co-op 🏭 Developer Pathea Games 📅 Release November 2023 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox, Switch

docs.title: 🔧 Workshop System
docs.text: Unlike traditional farming sims, Sandrock revolves around your Workshop — a crafting-based business. You accept commissions from townsfolk, build machines like furnaces and grinders, and manufacture everything from simple nails to advanced engines. Your workshop rank determines access to better blueprints and higher-paying commissions.

docs.title: Core Workshop Machines
docs.text: Machine Materials Needed Purpose ⌛ Furnace Copper ore + Stone Smelt basic bars ⚙️ Grinder Wood + Stone Process sand and ores 🔩 Assembly Station Iron bars + Circuits Build complex items 🪚 Worktable Wood Craft starter parts

docs.title: 🏙️ Desert Setting & Exploration
docs.text:
Sandrock's harsh desert environment is central to the experience. Water is scarce and must be managed carefully — you'll need a dew collector for hydration and a reliable supply chain for materials. The world is filled with ancient ruins, hazardous ruins for combat-based loot runs, and scattered oases that serve as rest points.

Tip: In the first week, focus entirely on filling commissions for the town board rather than hoarding materials. Each successful commission raises your workshop rank and unlocks better blueprints exponentially faster than free-building. Accept every commission you can physically fulfill.

docs.title: My Time at Sandrock Workshop Starter Guide
🔧 My Time at Sandrock — Workshop Starter Guide
Setting Up Your Workshop
docs.text: Your journey begins with a dilapidated workshop and a stack of debts. Your first task is to convert scrap metal and old wood into functional machinery. The tutorial walks you through building your first Worktable and Stone Furnace — these are your lifelines for the first week.

docs.title: Initial Build Order
docs.text: Priority Item Why 1 Worktable Enables basic crafting 2 Stone Furnace Smelt copper for tool upgrades 3 Dew Collector Passive water generation 4 Wooden Chest Extra storage for materials

docs.title: 💰 Earning Gols (Money)
docs.text: The primary income in Sandrock comes from commissions. Check the Commerce Commission Board daily for available jobs. Early commissions typically ask for basic items like copper bars, wooden sticks, or simple mechanisms.

docs.title: Best Early Money Methods
docs.text: Method Gols/Day Risk 📋 Town Commissions 200-800 Low — guaranteed payment 🏚️ Ruins Salvage 300-600 Medium — combat required 🌵 Foraging (Cactus, Sandcoral) 100-300 Low — free resources ⛏️ Mining + Selling Excess Ore 150-400 Low — always profitable

docs.title: 🛠️ Tool Upgrades
docs.text:
Your tools — pickaxe, axe, and hoe — can be upgraded at the Blacksmith. Each upgrade level increases efficiency dramatically.

Level Cost Benefit Copper 20 Copper bars 2x gather speed Iron 30 Iron bars 2x gather speed, can break desert rocks Stainless 50 Stainless bars 3x gather speed + larger area

Tip: Always keep at least 5 bronze bars in your inventory for emergency commissions. Many early board requests are time-sensitive and missing a deadline hurts your reputation. If you're short on materials, the scrap piles around town respawn daily and are completely free to salvage.

My Time at Sandrock — NPC Gift Guide

docs.title: Gift Giving Tips
docs.text:
• Loved gifts give the highest relationship boost — always check an NPC's Loved items before visiting.
  • Birthday gifts provide a massive 3x multiplier on relationship points.
  • Item quality matters — higher-quality versions of Loved items grant additional points.
  • Weekly limits apply: you can give each NPC up to 2 gifts per week (1 for spouses).
  • Commission rewards sometimes include exclusive Loved gifts for specific NPCs.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Water & Fuel
docs.text:
Water is the most critical resource in Sandrock. It is used for farming, crafting, and hydration.

Resource Source Primary Use Dew Drops Grass, Early Morning Harvest Minimal Water, Early Farming Water Bottle Water Tower, Purchase Farming, Cooking Large Water Tank Water Tower (upgraded) Bulk Water Storage Portable Water Tank Purchase from Water World Water on the Go Wood Chopping Trees Furnace Fuel Hardwood Chopping Large Trees Efficient Furnace Fuel Coal Mining (Gecko Station+), Purchase High-Efficiency Fuel Power Stones Mining, Monster Drops Machine Power Source

docs.title: Gathering Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
1. Upgrade your pickaxe and axe as soon as possible — higher tiers unlock access to rarer ores and woods.
  2. Scavenge the Breach daily for early-game scrap and data disks — it refreshes each in-game day.
  3. Plant Sandrice and Cactus on your farm for a renewable source of plant materials.
  4. Save Titanium and Magnesium ores for alloy production rather than selling raw — refined materials are worth significantly more.
  5. Check the train that passes through Sandrock — sometimes it drops rare materials on the tracks.
  6. Commission board rewards often include rare materials that are difficult to mine — prioritize these commissions.
  7. Geode processing at the Grinder station yields gems and rare crafting components — always crack geodes open rather than selling them.

Data source: My Time at Sandrock Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Stardew Valley Complete Boots & Footwear Guide
👢 Complete Boots & Footwear Guide
docs.text:
Boots are an essential equipment slot that provides defense (reduces damage taken) and immunity (reduces debuff duration). This guide covers every pair of boots available in Stardew Valley 1.6, from the humble Sneakers to the endgame Cinderclown Shoes.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📊 Boots Comparison Table
docs.text: Boots Defense Immunity How to Get Sell Price Rating Sneakers +1 −1 Starting equipment 50g ⭐⭐ Rubber Boots +1 −1 Mines F10 reward (chest) 50g ⭐⭐ Leather Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Work Boots +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (500g) 100g ⭐⭐ Combat Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (1,500g) 250g ⭐⭐⭐ Tundra Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (750g) 150g ⭐⭐ Thermal Boots +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (1,000g) 125g ⭐⭐⭐ Dark Boots +2 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,000g) 300g ⭐⭐⭐ Firewalker Boots +3 +3 Adventurer's Guild (4,000g) 500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Genie Shoes +1 −1 Adventurer's Guild (2,500g) 250g ⭐⭐ Cowboy Boots +3 −1 Adventurer's Guild (5,000g) 450g ⭐⭐⭐ Darker Boots +4 −1 Adventurer's Guild (6,000g) 600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Crystal Shoes +3 +5 Adventurer's Guild (10,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mermaid Boots +5 +5 Adventurer's Guild (20,000g) 1,200g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Dragonscale Boots +7 +7 Adventurer's Guild (25,000g) 1,500g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Space Boots +4 +4 Adventurer's Guild (15,000g) 750g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Leprechaun Shoes +1 +1 Adventurer's Guild (3,000g) 200g ⭐⭐ Cinderclown Shoes +6 +6 Combat Mastery (1.6) 1,600g ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Early Game (Years 1–2)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Leather Boots First upgrade from Sneakers Buy at Adventurer's Guild for 500g — cheap defense boost Combat Boots +3 defense for the mid-Mines Best value upgrade at 1,500g — carry to floor 80+ Thermal Boots +1 immunity against slime debuffs Good for Slime Hutch and Quarry Mine runs

docs.title: Mid Game (Year 2–3)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Firewalker Boots +3 defense & immunity Excellent all-rounder for Skull Cavern early runs Crystal Shoes +3 defense, +5 immunity Best mid-game boots — immunity to most debuffs Space Boots +4 defense & immunity Solid stats, reasonable price (15,000g)

docs.title: Late Game (Year 3+)
docs.text: Boots Why Get It Strategy Dragonscale Boots +7 defense & immunity Second-best in game — 25,000g Cinderclown Shoes +6 defense & immunity Free (Combat Mastery) — requires Mastery Cave in Cindersap Mermaid Boots +5 defense & immunity Budget alternative at 20,000g

docs.title: 📊 Boots Stat Evolution
docs.text:
This chart shows how defense and immunity improve as you progress:

Defense:  Sneakers (1) → Combat (3) → Firewalker (3) → Crystal (3) → Space (4) → Cinderclown (6) → Dragonscale (7)
Immunity: Sneakers (−1) → Thermal (+1) → Firewalker (+3) → Space (+4) → Crystal (+5) → Cinderclown (+6) → Dragonscale (+7)

Key insight: Immunity is more valuable than defense in the late game. Debuffs from serpents, slimes, and ghosts are the main cause of death — not raw damage.

docs.title: 🏆 Boot Progression Roadmap
docs.text:
1. Day 1: Sneakers (+1/−1) — all you need for the first mines run
  2. Floor 10: Check for Rubber Boots in the chest — free upgrade
  3. Guild unlock (Slime kills x10): Buy Combat Boots (1,500g) — best early investment
  4. After reaching floor 80: Save for Firewalker Boots (4,000g)
  5. Deeper Mines clear: Save 15,000g for Space Boots or 20,000g for Mermaid Boots
  6. Mastery Cave: Claim free Cinderclown Shoes
  7. Endgame: Dragonscale Boots (25,000g) — final pair, only if you have gold to spare

docs.title: 💡 Tips
docs.text:
• Boots never break — buy the best you can afford, they last forever
  • Cinderclown Shoes are the only boots that don't cost money (Combat Mastery reward)
  • Immunity reduces the duration of: Slime debuff (slow), Ghost debuff (dark), Bat debuff (speed down), and Serpent debuff (defense down)
  • Defense reduces damage by roughly 1 HP per point, so +7 defense = 7 less damage per hit
  • You can find rare boot drops in Volcano Dungeon treasure rooms — always check them

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6. All boot stats, prices, and locations verified for version 1.6.9.

docs.title: Top 5 Keg / Preserves Profit
docs.text:
Crop Base Price Product Product Price Multiplier Starfruit ⭐ 750g Starfruit Wine 🍷 2250g 3x Melon 🍈 250g Melon Wine 🍷 750g 3x Pumpkin 🎃 320g Pumpkin Juice 🧃 720g 2.25x Hops 🌿 25g Pale Ale 🍺 300g 12x Wheat 🌾 10g Beer 🍺 200g 20x

Hops are the Keg efficiency king! Though the base price is low, they're harvested daily, take 11 days to mature, then produce every day — a year-round Keg assembly line.

Note: Fruits go into Kegs to make Wine (3x base price). Vegetables go into Kegs to make Juice (2.25x base price). Pumpkin is a vegetable, so it produces Pumpkin Juice, not wine. Blueberries (fruit) produce Blueberry Wine at 150g (50g × 3).

docs.title: Greenhouse Planting Recommendations
docs.text:
Priority Crop Reason 1 Starfruit ⭐ Grow year-round, Keg and sell 2 Ancient Fruit 🍇 Single seed, regrows every 7 days forever 3 Hops 🌿 Daily harvest, huge Keg profit 4 Coffee Bean ☕ Mass plant, drink coffee daily for speed boost 5 Blueberry 🫐 Regrows, low effort 6 Cranberry 🫐 Regrows, winter supplement

💡 Beginner Tip: In the first year, Spring is mainly about making money by fishing. Only grow enough parsnips to complete the bundle, save your gold for Strawberries (buy at Egg Festival on Spring 13).

💡 Intermediate Tip: Once you unlock the Greenhouse, immediately plant Starfruit + Hops in it. With Kegs, earning over 10k/day is easy.

💡 1.6 New Crop Tip: Seed Spot crops (Carrot, Summer Squash, Broccoli, Powdermelon) are bonus income — no gold investment needed. Powdermelon in Winter is the only seasonal Winter crop and can form giant crops!

docs.title: 🏛️ Stardew Valley Fair — 1000 Star Tokens Strategy
docs.text:
The Summer 16 Stardew Valley Fair is your chance to get rare items. Here's how to get 1000 tokens easily:

Best Approach: Fishing mini-game - Takes ~5 minutes at the fishing booth - Easy to score 100+ tokens per attempt with practice - No need to save items all year

Alternative (Item-Based): Bring high-quality items for the grange display: - 9 Items × iridium quality + varied categories = max points - Diamond, Wine, Gold Bar, Iridium-quality crops

Best Purchase: Stardrop (2,000 tokens) — permanent energy boost. Save your tokens for this.

docs.title: 💰 Fall Profit Maximization
docs.text:
Activity Daily Gold Notes Cranberries (200 plants) ~3,000g Continuous harvest, low effort Pumpkin (100) + Keg ~2,500g High profit per plant Ancient Fruit + Keg ~4,000g If unlocked from greenhouse Preserves Jars (Cranberries) +40% value Continuous input Kegs (Hops saved from Summer) ~500g/day Already grown, just process

Bottom Line: Cranberries for steady passive income + Pumpkins (kegged) for big batches = optimal Fall economy. Don't forget to plant corn on Summer 28! It survives into Fall for a head start.

docs.title: 🏠 Farm Layout Planning
docs.text: A good layout = time-saving + profitable + beautiful. This guide provides layout plans for every stage from beginner to endgame.

docs.title: Golden Rules of Layout
docs.text: Rule Description 1. Centralized Irrigation Concentrate farming in one area for maximum sprinkler coverage 2. Optimal Pathing Daily route: House → Mailbox → Farmhouse → Greenhouse → Barn, in a straight line 3. Modular Design Four main zones: Planting Zone + Animal Zone + Processing Zone + Decoration Zone 4. Leave Room Buildings can be moved, but plan roads and tree positions in advance

docs.title: 🌱 Beginner Layout (First Year Spring)
docs.text:
Goal: Maximize planting area with minimal money

┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│       Farm Entrance               │
│   ┌──────┐                       │
│   │ House │                      │
│   └──────┘                       │
│        │                         │
│     ┌──┴────────────┐            │
│     │  Small Plot    │  ← Hand-water │
│     │  (40 tiles)    │            │
│     │  Parsnip+Potato│            │
│     └───────────────┘            │
│        │                         │
│     ┌──┴────────────┐            │
│     │  Chests+Boxes  │           │
│     │  Furnace+Anvil │           │
│     └───────────────┘            │
│                                  │
│   🐔 Coop (build later)          │
└─────────────────────────────────┘

First Spring Focus: Don't worry about layout — earn money and upgrade tools first. Save enough by the end of spring to buy Copper/Iron Sprinklers.

docs.title: 💰 Economic Layout (First Year Summer)
docs.text:
Goal: Sprinkler coverage + Maximize planting income

┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│   House  │                     │
│          │                     │
│   ┌──────┴──────────────┐      │
│   │ Copper Sprinkler     │      │
│   │  ★  ★  ★           │      │
│   │ (3×3 each, covers 8) │ ← Key │
│   └─────────────────────┘      │
│          │                     │
│  ┌───────┴──────┐  ┌────┴────┐│
│  │ Blueberries   │  │ Hops×24 ││
│  │ ×40           │  │ For wine││
│  │ Money King    │  │ making  ││
│  └──────────────┘  └─────────┘│
│          │                     │
│  ┌───────┴──────┐              │
│  │ Coop×1       │ ← Eggs for  │
│  │ Barn×1       │   profit    │
│  └──────────────┘              │
└─────────────────────────────────┘

Key: Before summer ends, have at least 8 Copper Sprinklers (covering 64 tiles) so you won't need to hand-water in fall.

docs.text:
Path Keg Keg Keg Path Keg Keg Keg Path

→ Corridor → Two rows with one aisle — won't block when placing/collecting wine

docs.title: Auto-Grabber Path
docs.text:
Coop/Barn entrance → Auto-Grabber (purchased from Robin)
→ Load directly into processing machines → Products go to chests
Fully hands-free

docs.title: Farm Type Selection Guide
docs.text:
Farm Type Best For Difficulty Standard Farm Beginners ✅ Most space ⭐ Easy Riverland Farm Fishing enthusiasts 🎣 ⭐⭐ Forest Farm Foraging fans 🌲 ⭐⭐ Hill-top Farm Miners ⛏️ ⭐⭐⭐ Wilderness Farm Combat lovers ⚔️ ⭐⭐⭐ Four Corners Farm Multiplayer/Modular ⭐ Beach Farm Challenge players 💪 ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Beginner recommendation: Standard Farm or Four Corners Farm.

docs.title: ❓ Layout FAQ
docs.text:
Q: Do I need to plan the layout all at once? A: No. Buildings can be moved by Robin (free!), but roads and trees can be planned in advance.

Q: When should I start planning the final layout? A: First year winter — by then all the land is cleared and you have some money to spare.

Q: When can I get lots of Iridium Sprinklers? A: After reaching floor 80+ in the mines, open geodes and kill monsters for iridium ore, then craft iridium sprinklers.

docs.title: Stardew Valley Festival Events Calendar — Complete Year Guide
🎪 Festival Events Calendar
docs.text:
Stardew Valley has 12 major festivals each year, plus the 3-day Night Market. Every festival offers unique activities, rewards, and seasonal shopping opportunities. This guide lists every festival with complete details.

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6

docs.title: 📅 Quick Reference: Complete Festival Calendar
docs.text: Date Festival Season Location Type Best For Spring 13 Egg Festival 🌸 Spring Town Center Contest + Shop 🥇 Strawberry Seeds Spring 24 Flower Dance 🌸 Spring Cindersap Forest Social + Shop 💞 Romance opportunity Summer 11-13 Night Market ☀️ Summer Beach Shop + Fishing 🐟 Deep-sea fish + Pearl Summer 28 Moonlight Jellies ☀️ Summer Beach Watching 🌙 Decoration reward Fall 16 Stardew Valley Fair 🍁 Fall Town Center Contest + Mini-games 🥇 Stardrop + Star Tokens Fall 27 Spirit's Eve 🍁 Fall Town Square Maze + Shop 🎃 Golden Pumpkin Winter 8 Fishing Tournament ❄️ Winter Beach Contest 🎣 Iridium Rod + Hat Winter 15-17 Night Market ❄️ Winter Beach Shop + Fishing 🐟 Deep-sea fish (repeat!) Winter 25 Feast of the Winter Star ❄️ Winter Lewis' House Social + Gift 🎁 Rare gift items — Desert Festival All Year Desert Shop 🐪 Rare seeds + items

docs.title: Spring 13 — Egg Festival
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Town Center (near Community Center) Start Location Talk to Lewis at the Town Center Main Activity Egg Hunt — find 9 hidden eggs in the town Win Condition Find the most eggs (Abigail is the NPC to beat) Win Reward Straw Hat (cosmetic) Participation Reward Straw Hat (win or lose — you still get one)

🛒 Festival Shop (Must-Buy Items)

Item Price Priority Notes Strawberry Seeds 100g each ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Most profitable spring crop — buy 20-50+ Straw Hat 2,000g ⭐⭐ Already earned from participation Decorations 500-1,000g ⭐ Cosmetic only

💰 Strawberry Seed Investment Guide

Playstyle Seeds to Buy Expected Harvests Profit First Year (Spring 13 planted) 20-30 packs 2 harvests (Spring 16 + 20) ~2,000-3,000g First Year (Spring 13, Speed-Gro) 40-50 packs 3 harvests (Spring 16, 20, 24) ~6,000-8,000g Second Year+ 100-200+ packs 4-5 harvests with Deluxe Speed-Gro Massive profit

docs.title: Spring 24 — Flower Dance
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Cindersap Forest (south of the Wizard's Tower) Start Location Enter the forest — automatically starts Main Activity Dance with an NPC (requires ≥4 hearts) Reward None (cosmetic only) Secondary Activities Shop for decorations + flowers

💞 How to Dance with an NPC

  1. Give 2 quality gifts per week (Loved gifts preferred)
  2. Talk to them daily
  3. By Spring 24, you need 4 hearts
  4. Easiest NPCs to dance with Year 1:
  5. Haley — Loves Daffodils (foraged) and Sunflowers
  6. Elliott — Loves Duck Feathers or Pomegranate (harder Year 1)
  7. Leah — Loves Salad (buy at Saloon for 220g)
  8. Harvey — Loves Coffee (buy at Saloon for 300g)

Note: Dancing with an NPC gives a massive friendship boost — even if you just stand there awkwardly.

docs.title: Summer 11-13 — Night Market (Important!)
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM (runs 3 days) Location The Beach Main Attractions Submarine ride, Mermaid show, Shops Key Items Rare fish, Pearl, Rare Seeds, Paintings

🎯 Night Market Must-Do Checklist (Daily)

Task Day 1 Day 2 Day 3 🐟 Ride Submarine ✅ ✅ ✅ 🐚 Watch Mermaid Show ✅ — — 🛍️ Buy Rare Seeds ✅ ✅ ✅ 🎨 Buy Paintings ✅ ✅ ✅ ☕ Buy Coffee (1,000g for 100 cups) ✅ ✅ ✅ 🪨 Buy Artifact Trove ✅ ✅ ✅

🐟 Submarine Fish Catalog

Fish Sell Price Difficulty Time Available Blobfish 500g ⭐⭐ All 3 days Spookfish 220g ⭐⭐ All 3 days Midnight Squid 250g ⭐⭐⭐ All 3 days Sea Cucumber 150g ⭐ All 3 days Super Cucumber 250g ⭐⭐⭐ All 3 days Scorpion Carp (rare) 450g ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Rare

💰 Pearl Acquisition (Mermaid Show) - Watch the complete mermaid show - Get Pearl (worth 2,500g) - Pearl can also be crafted into Wedding Ring (multiplayer) or sold

docs.title: Summer 28 — Moonlight Jellies Festival
docs.text: Detail Info Time 7:00 PM – 12:00 AM Location The Beach Main Activity Watch the moonlight jelly migration Reward Moonlight Jelly Decoration (unique furniture) Best Strategy Just enjoy the view — it's a relaxing end-of-season event

docs.title: Fall 16 — Stardew Valley Fair (Most Important Festival!)
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM Location Town Center Main Activity Grange Display contest + Mini-games + Shopping Win Condition Highest score in the Grange Display 1st Place Prize 1,000 Star Tokens

🏆 Grange Display Scoring Guide

Category Max Points Best Items to Use Base Score (9 items) 9 points Fill all 9 slots! Category Diversity Up to 30 points Use items from 6+ different categories Item Quality Up to 28 points Iridium quality items give max points Legendary Fish Up to 8 points Legendary fish give huge bonus

🏅 Best Grange Display Combinations

Slot Best Item (Early Game) Best Item (Late Game) 1 Gold-quality Melon Iridium Ancient Fruit 2 Gold-quality Pumpkin Iridium Wine 3 Gold-quality Cheese Iridium Cheese 4 Gold-quality Mayonnaise Iridium Mayonnaise 5 Diamond Diamond 6 Gold-quality Fish (Largemouth Bass) Legendary Fish 7 Gold-quality Forage (Morel) Iridium Forage 8 Gold-quality Egg (Large) Iridium Dinosaur Egg 9 Gold-quality Milk (Large) Iridium Goat Cheese

🪙 Star Token Shop — Purchase Priority

Item Price Priority Notes Stardrop 2,000 Tokens 🥇 MUST BUY Permanently +34 max energy Rare Seed 100 Tokens each 🥇 BUY 5-10 Grows Sweet Gem Berry (3,000g sell) Farm Computer 2,000 Tokens 🥈 Shows farm stats Coffee Maker 2,000 Tokens 🥈 Daily coffee (1.6 buffed) Knight's Helmet 2,000 Tokens 🥉 Cosmetic Hats 1,000 Tokens ⭐ Cosmetic Halloween Decorations 500 Tokens ⭐ Cosmetic

docs.title: Fall 27 — Spirit's Eve
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 10:00 AM – 11:50 PM Location Town Square Main Activity Maze exploration Reward Golden Pumpkin (sell for 2,500g or use as decoration)

🎃 Maze Guide 1. Enter the maze house (looks like a haunted mansion) 2. Navigate through dark corridors 3. Find the exit — the Golden Pumpkin is at the end 4. You can also buy Halloween decorations at the shop

docs.title: Winter 8 — Festival of Ice (Fishing Tournament)
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Beach (frozen river) Main Activity Ice fishing competition — catch the biggest fish! Win Condition Catch the heaviest fish (length or weight) 1st Place Prize Choice of Iridium Rod, Fisherman's Hat, Sailor's Cap Strategy Stock up a big fish in advance OR fish early that morning

🎣 How to Win (Guaranteed)

Method Details Method 1 (Cheese) Catch a Legendary Fish earlier in the year, save it in a chest, then enter it at the festival = instant win Method 2 (Day-of) Fish in the Mountain Lake before the festival — catch a Sturgeon or large Largemouth Bass Method 3 (Natural) Compete with the AI — you usually only need a 10+ inch fish to win

docs.title: Winter 15-17 — Night Market (Repeat!)
docs.text: Same as Summer Night Market — see above for details. This is your second chance to catch deep-sea fish and buy rare seeds.

docs.title: Winter 25 — Feast of the Winter Star
docs.text:
Detail Info Time 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM Location Lewis' House Main Activity Gift exchange + Feast Mechanic Each player is assigned a secret friend to give a gift to

🎁 Secret Friend Gift Guide

Recipient Best Gift (Loved) Alternative (Liked) Any Villager Best: Prismatic Shard Good: Diamond, Gold Bar Abigail Amethyst, Pumpkin Quartz Emily Cloth, Gem Wool Haley Sunflower, Coconut Daffodil Leah Salad, Wine Cheese Penny Poppy, Diamond Leek Maru Gold Bar, Battery Strawberry Alex Complete Breakfast Salmon Dinner Elliot Pomegranate, Duck Feather Crab Cakes Harvey Coffee, Wine Pickles Sam Pizza, Cactus Fruit Joja Cola Sebastian Frozen Tear, Sashimi Obsidian Shane Pizza, Beer Hot Pepper Clint Amethyst, Gold Bar Geode Willy Catfish, Diamond Fish Marnie Diamond, Farmer's Lunch Pumpkin Pie Pam Pale Ale, Glazed Yams Potato Juice Jodi Pancakes, Diamond Vegetable Soup Lewis Glazed Yams, Hot Pepper Vegetable Medley Robin Goat Cheese, Peach Wood Demetrius Strawberry, Bean Hotpot Rice Pudding Linus Blueberry Tart, Coconut Wild Horseradish Gus Diamond, Fish Taco Salmon Dinner Wizard Purple Mushroom, Void Essence Solar Essence

docs.title: 🌵 Desert Festival
docs.text: Detail Info Location The Desert (west side) When Any season, after bus is repaired Key Shop Desert Trader Rare Items Desert Totem recipe, Magic Rock Candy, Rare Seeds

docs.title: Year 1 Must-Do Festivals
docs.text: Priority Festival Why Preparation 🥇 Spring 13 — Egg Festival ✅ Strawberry Seeds (best spring crop) Save 2,000-5,000g 🥇 Summer 11-13 — Night Market ✅ Deep-sea fish + Pearl Iridium Rod + bait 🥇 Fall 16 — Stardew Valley Fair ✅ Stardrop + Star Tokens Grange display items + pumpkins 🥇 Winter 15-17 — Night Market ✅ Deep-sea fish again Iridium Rod + bait 🥇 Winter 25 — Feast of Winter Star ✅ Rare gift items Keep a diamond/prismatic shard 🥈 Fall 27 — Spirit's Eve ✅ Golden Pumpkin Just show up 🥈 Winter 8 — Fishing Tournament ✅ Iridium Rod Save a big fish

docs.title: Annual Income from Festivals
docs.text:
Festival Direct Gold Value Indirect Value Spring 13 0g +5,000-15,000g from strawberry harvests Summer 11-13 2,500g (Pearl) +3,000g from deep-sea fish Fall 16 Unlimited (Stardrop + items) Farming knowledge Fall 27 2,500g (Golden Pumpkin) — Winter 15-17 2,500g (Pearl again) +3,000g from deep-sea fish

Data from Stardew Valley Wiki v1.6. All festival dates, times, and rewards verified for version 1.6.9.

docs.title: Money-Making Tips
docs.text:
Priority Fish Reason 🥇 Spring: Catfish 200g each, more river fish in rain 🥈 Summer: Octopus 150g each, but only appears 6AM-1PM 🥉 Year-round: Sturgeon 200g each, pond produces Roe→Aged Roe

Fishing is the most profitable Year 1 skill. Grab the rod on Spring 1, fish every day until 2PM, earning 500-1000g/day easily.

💡 Fish Pond advice: Stock Sturgeon (produces Roe for Caviar) or Squid/Sea Cucumber (for cooking gifts).

docs.title: 🎣 Complete Fishing Guide
docs.text: Fishing is one of the most profitable skills in Stardew Valley — and the fastest way to earn money early on. This guide covers the complete path from fishing novice to legendary fish hunter.

docs.title: Fishing Level Unlocks
docs.text: Level Unlock Description 1 Training Rod 25g, lower quality catch but easier to use 2 Bait Attach bait to rod to reduce bite time 3 Crab Pot Recipe Place crab pots in water to catch creatures 4 Recycling Machine Recycle trash into resources 5 Fisher/Gatherer Fisher: Fish sell for 25% more / Gatherer: Double crab pot yield 6 Spinner + Bait Spinner reduces fish struggle time 7 Treasure Hunter Increases treasure chest spawn rate 8 Cork Bobber Increases fishing bar size 9 Magnet Attracts more treasure chests 10 Mariner/Pirate Mariner: Crab pots catch exclusive fish / Pirate: Double treasure chest chance

docs.title: Fishing Rod Recommendations
docs.text: Rod Price Required Level Description Bamboo Pole Free Starting Can't attach bait Training Rod 25g 1 Good for beginners to practice Fiberglass Rod 1800g 2 Can attach bait Iridium Rod 7500g 6 Can attach bait + tackle Space Rod ? Late game All-purpose rod

docs.title: Days 1-3 (Beginner Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Bamboo Pole
Location: Ocean Pier
Strategy: Catch Sardines + Anchovies (easy to catch)
Income:   500-800g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 2

docs.title: Days 4-7 (Apprentice Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Fiberglass Rod + Bait
Location: Lake/River
Strategy: Bait speeds up bite time
Income:   1000-2000g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 5

docs.title: Days 8-14 (Skilled Phase)
docs.text:
Gear:     Iridium Rod + Bait + Tackle
Location: Rotate River + Ocean + Lake
Strategy: Catch high-value fish for the season
Income:   3000-5000g/day
Goal:     Reach Fishing Level 10

docs.title: Spring
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Catfish (Rainy days, River)
    Price: 200g+   Difficulty: Medium
    Pair with: Copper pan coffee + Trap Bobber

docs.title: Summer
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Octopus (6AM-1PM, Ocean)
    Price: 300g+   Difficulty: Extreme
    Recommended: Iridium Rod + Trap Bobber

docs.title: Fall
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Walleye (Rainy night)
    Price: 300g+   Difficulty: Hard
    Pair with: Spinner + Bait

docs.title: Winter
docs.text:
⭐ Most Profitable: Glacierfish (Seasonal Legendary)
    Price: 1000g+   Difficulty: Extreme
    One chance — make sure you catch it!

docs.title: Fast Leveling Method
docs.text:
1. Carp (Lake) + Sunfish give 50 XP → catch 50 to level up
2. Lucky days (TV fortune) increase treasure chest chance
3. Perfect catches give +20% bonus XP
4. Eat Fish Stew (+1 Fishing Level)
5. Rainy days = more rare fish

docs.title: Best Equipment Combo
docs.text:
Iridium Rod + Bait + Trap Bobber
= 50% faster bite time + 33% slower fish escape speed

docs.title: Legendary Fish Tips
docs.text:
1. Save in advance (after waking up)
  2. Eat Seafoam Pudding (+4 Fishing Level)
  3. Use Trap Bobber (not Lead Bobber)
  4. Stay calm — steady hands matter most

docs.title: 🌱 Greenhouse Crops
